skill.name: container-management
skill.description: Create, manage, and optimize LXC containers in Proxmox. Control container lifecycle, manage resources, and coordinate deployments across nodes.
skill.summary: Manage Proxmox LXC containers, lifecycle actions, resource settings, templates, and placement planning.
skill.author: DataKnifeAI

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution, weak-crypto, and reconnaissance findings were reviewed against SKILL.md and are false positives caused by documentation text. The skill still carries medium operational risk because it describes Proxmox container lifecycle actions, including stop, delete, clone, and create operations.

medium_findings.title: Destructive Container Operations Require Review
medium_findings.description: The skill documents container lifecycle tools that can stop, reboot, clone, create, and delete LXC containers. This is legitimate Proxmox administration functionality, but misuse could interrupt services or remove data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented tool names and workflows directly include lifecycle and deletion actions. The risk is operational rather than malicious because the file presents normal container administration guidance.

low_findings.title: Markdown Tool Names Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the referenced lines are markdown code spans around tool names and workflow steps. No executable Ruby, shell command, or dynamic command construction is present in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is visibly markdown documentation, not source code. The file contains no Ruby backtick syntax in an executable context.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are Text Matches Only
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but the cited lines contain the skill description, a container listing bullet, and a best-practice sentence. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing code, or encryption operation appears at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are ordinary prose about containers and node balancing. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or similar weak crypto usage.

low_findings.title: Hostname Reference Is Benign Documentation
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance finding points to a best-practice recommendation to use meaningful hostnames. This is normal operational documentation and does not collect, transmit, enumerate, or expose host data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a static documentation recommendation, not executable reconnaissance logic. No scanning or exfiltration behavior is present.

content.value_statement: Proxmox container administration requires accurate status checks and careful lifecycle changes. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code manage LXC containers with structured workflows.
actual_capabilities: Lists containers on a selected Proxmox node.
Retrieves container status, configuration, and resource details.
Guides start, stop, shutdown, reboot, clone, delete, and create workflows.
Supports basic and advanced LXC container creation planning.
Helps review resource allocation, templates, and placement across nodes.
Provides troubleshooting prompts for container health and configuration issues.
limitations: Requires compatible Proxmox tooling and permissions outside the skill file.
Does not include implementation code for the listed tools.
Cannot guarantee safe deletion or shutdown without operator confirmation.
Does not replace backups, snapshots, monitoring, or change management.

use_cases.title: Review Container Fleet Status
use_cases.description: Check which containers are running, stopped, or misconfigured before a maintenance window.

use_cases.title: Provision Test Containers
use_cases.description: Create or clone LXC containers with planned CPU, memory, storage, and template choices.

use_cases.title: Optimize Node Placement
use_cases.description: Compare container resource usage and plan balanced placement across Proxmox nodes.

prompt_templates.title: List Containers
prompt_templates.prompt: List all LXC containers on node NODE_NAME and summarize their current state and resource allocation.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect One Container
prompt_templates.prompt: Get the status and full configuration for container VMID on node NODE_NAME. Highlight resource limits and unusual settings.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Safe Lifecycle Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a safe shutdown or reboot plan for container VMID. Include checks before the action and validation after it completes.

prompt_templates.title: Design Container Deployment
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a new LXC container deployment for WORKLOAD_NAME with CPU, memory, storage, template, hostname, and placement recommendations.

output_examples.input: List containers on the worker node.
output: A concise table of container identifiers, names, status, CPU, memory, and storage allocation.
A short summary of containers that need attention.

output_examples.input: Create a plan for a 2 core, 4 GB application container.
output: Recommended CPU, memory, storage, template, hostname, and network choices.
Validation steps to confirm the container starts and has expected resources.

output_examples.input: Troubleshoot container 105 after a failed reboot.
output: Status checks, configuration review points, and likely resource or startup causes.
A recovery sequence that starts with non-destructive checks.

best_practices: Confirm node name, container ID, and intended action before lifecycle changes.
Use graceful shutdown and snapshots before disruptive or destructive operations.
Document resource allocations, templates, hostnames, and container purpose.
anti_patterns: Deleting containers without verifying backups and ownership.
Changing CPU, memory, or storage limits without checking workload needs.
Cloning production containers into test environments without reviewing secrets.

faq.question: Does this skill create containers by itself?
faq.answer: It documents workflows and tool usage. Actual creation depends on connected Proxmox tools and permissions.

faq.question: Can it manage containers across multiple nodes?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill describes listing, placement planning, and lifecycle workflows across Proxmox nodes.

faq.question: Is this safe for production environments?
faq.answer: It can be used in production with approvals, backups, and operator review for disruptive actions.

faq.question: Does it support advanced container settings?
faq.answer: Yes. It references advanced container creation and configuration review workflows.

faq.question: Can it troubleshoot container failures?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides status checks, configuration review, metrics inspection, and recovery planning.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk medium?
faq.answer: The file is documentation, but it covers infrastructure actions that can stop services or delete containers.
